positioning: Doctor-formulated Ayurvedic obesity and weight-loss system (structured guided system; not a loose supplement).
Not positioned as a standalone fat-burner claim product.
promise_claims: 3 to 5 kg weight loss in 15 days when protocol is followed.
eligibility: Adults with BMI above 25 who want fast visible weight-loss support with natural and practical adherence.
caution_conditions: Doctor-review required before use for people on multiple psychiatric medications.
Doctor-review required before use for people with multiple surgeries.
protocol_morning: Take OK Liquid in the morning on an empty stomach.
protocol_night: Take OK Tablet plus 1/2 tsp OK Powder around 15 minutes before sleep with warm water.
fasting_rules: After OK Liquid, keep a 4-hour no-solid-food window.
Zero-calorie drinks are allowed during the no-solids window.
allowed_items: Up to 30g complex carbs (millets or oats) if still hungry.
Milk in tea/coffee (as the only dairy exception stated).
mechanism_allowed_claims: Protocol-based appetite regulation + eating-behavior correction + digestion support for obesity/weight-loss support.
Supports fullness/satiety and reduces cravings to make true-hunger eating easier.
Supports digestive comfort (bloating, acidity, gas, heaviness) to help routine consistency.
Supports weight-loss efforts by making caloric-deficit adherence easier in real life.
safety_internal_handling: Internal handling: first 3 days can be an adjustment phase.
guarantee: If weight-loss start is not observed in the first 7 days, refund applies only when protocol is followed and daily tracker is shared.
continuation: Continue in cycles until target weight or BMI 25.
After every 30 days take a 7 to 10 day break.
maintenance: Maintain caloric balance, balanced diet, and daily activity.
No automatic bounce back from stopping alone.
proof_points: 7-day refund policy (conditioned on protocol + daily tracker sharing).
messaging_guardrails: Do not use abstract copy that hides the obesity/weight-loss objective.
on_image_copy_requirements: Every headline and subheadline must explicitly mention weight loss, obesity reduction, or clear weight outcome language. Avoid abstract copy that hides the core product goal.
Approved explicit phrases include: weight loss support; obesity management support; lose weight; reduce excess weight; 3 to 5 kg in 15 days; appetite control for weight loss.
Do not use vague transformational lines without naming weight loss or obesity context.
Mechanism messaging must explicitly connect to obesity/weight-loss goal, not abstract behavior language alone.
